description: Drop-in CLI binary (codemux) that opens Zed terminals inside tmux/zellij — port of vscode-mux to Zed, packaged in the lean style of th0jensen/fff-gpui (pure Rust binary, no Zed extension manifest, integration via Zed settings.json or tasks.json + keymap.json). Mirrors vscode-mux session-naming and multi-window indexing exactly so the same workspace opened in VS Code and Zed lands in the same multiplexer session.

userStories.title: Scaffold the Rust binary crate
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working Rust crate so subsequent stories have a place to add code. Mirrors fff-gpui's layout: pure binary, no [lib], no extension manifest.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Cargo.toml at repo root with package name 'codemux', edition 2021, [[bin]] target named 'codemux' (no [lib] section)
Add baseline dependencies in Cargo.toml: clap (with derive feature), which, serde + serde_derive, toml, anyhow, regex, dirs
Create src/main.rs with a minimal entry point that prints 'codemux' and exits 0
Add .gitignore covering /target and .DS_Store
Add LICENSE file (MIT) at repo root with year 2026 and author 'jellydn'
`cargo build` succeeds
`cargo run` prints 'codemux' and exits 0
Typecheck passes (cargo check passes)
userStories.notes: Completed: Cargo.toml with [[bin]] target, all dependencies (clap with derive, which, serde, toml, anyhow, regex, dirs), src/main.rs, .gitignore with /target and .DS_Store, LICENSE (MIT 2026 jellydn). Build and tests pass.

userStories.title: Implement session-name sanitization with vscode-mux fixture tests
userStories.description: As the codemux binary, I need to convert any workspace name into a tmux/zellij-safe session name using the exact vscode-mux algorithm so cross-editor session sharing works.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/sanitize.rs with `pub fn sanitize_session_name(name: &str) -> String`
Algorithm exactly matches vscode-mux: replace any char not in [a-zA-Z0-9-] with '-', collapse consecutive '-' into one, strip leading/trailing '-', return literal 'session' if result is empty
Use the regex crate or hand-rolled char iteration

Unit tests cover the vscode-mux fixture table: 'My Workspace' → 'My-Workspace', 'my.project' → 'my-project', 'my_project' → 'my-project', 'my...project' → 'my-project', '-myproject-' → 'myproject', '...' → 'session', '' → 'session'
All unit tests pass (`cargo test sanitize`)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement POSIX shell-escape helper with tests
userStories.description: As the codemux binary, I need a shell-escape function (matching vscode-mux exactly) so session names and cwd values are safely interpolated when building shell commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/shell_escape.rs with `pub fn shell_escape(value: &str) -> String`
If input is empty, return literal `''`
Otherwise wrap in single quotes and replace internal `'` with `'"'"'` (POSIX single-quote escaping)
Unit tests: '' → "''", 'foo' → "'foo'", "it's" → "'it'\"'\"'s'", '/path with spaces' → "'/path with spaces'"
All unit tests pass (`cargo test shell_escape`)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement TOML config-file loader
userStories.description: As the codemux binary, I need to read ~/.config/codemux/config.toml so users can persist multiplexer choice and auto_attach.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/config.rs with a `Config` struct: `multiplexer: Option<String>`, `auto_attach: Option<bool>`
Function `pub fn load_config() -> Config` reads `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/codemux/config.toml` or `~/.config/codemux/config.toml` (Windows: `%APPDATA%/codemux/config.toml`) using the `dirs` crate
Returns default `Config { multiplexer: None, auto_attach: None }` if file is missing or unreadable (no panic)
Provide a `parse_config_str(input: &str) -> Config` helper used by tests so they don't touch the filesystem
Unit tests: parse a valid TOML string, parse an empty string (returns defaults), parse invalid TOML (returns defaults, does not panic)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement multiplexer detection (env → config → PATH)
userStories.description: As the codemux binary, I need to decide which multiplexer to use, in the priority order from the PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/detect.rs with `pub enum Multiplexer { Tmux, Zellij }` and `pub fn detect_multiplexer(config: &Config) -> Option<Multiplexer>`
Resolution order: 1) env var `CODEMUX_MULTIPLEXER` ('tmux' | 'zellij'), 2) config.multiplexer field, 3) PATH probe via `which` crate — prefer 'tmux', then 'zellij'
Returns `None` only when none of the above resolves
Unit tests inject env vars and config to verify resolution priority; PATH-probe path tests are `#[ignore]` if dependent on host state
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement MuxLauncher trait + tmux session listing & build_command
userStories.description: As the codemux binary, I need an abstraction for multiplexer operations and a tmux implementation that can list live sessions and build the right command string.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/launcher.rs with `pub trait MuxLauncher { fn list_sessions(&self) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<String>>; fn build_command(&self, name: &str, cwd: &str, auto_attach: bool) -> String; }`
Add src/tmux.rs implementing `MuxLauncher` for `TmuxLauncher`
`list_sessions` runs `tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}'`, captures stdout, splits by newline, trims, filters empty
If tmux exits non-zero (no server running), treat as empty session list (do not error)
`build_command` matches vscode-mux: auto-attach → `tmux new-session -A -s <escaped-name> -c <escaped-cwd>`; otherwise → `tmux new-session -s <escaped-name> -c <escaped-cwd>`
Unit test for `build_command` covers both auto-attach values
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement zellij launcher (list + build_command)
userStories.description: As the codemux binary, I need a zellij implementation of MuxLauncher matching vscode-mux's exact commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/zellij.rs implementing `MuxLauncher` for `ZellijLauncher`
`list_sessions` runs `zellij list-sessions -n`, captures stdout, splits/trims, filters empty
If zellij exits non-zero because no sessions exist, return empty list (do not error)
`build_command`: auto-attach → `zellij attach <escaped-name> -c`; otherwise → `zellij -s <escaped-name>` (NO -c <cwd> — matches vscode-mux behavior)
Unit tests for `build_command` cover both auto-attach values
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement get_unique_session_name with gap-filling
userStories.description: As the codemux binary, I need to compute a unique suffixed session name using the exact vscode-mux algorithm so multi-window scenarios behave identically across editors.
acceptanceCriteria: Add src/unique_name.rs with `pub fn get_unique_session_name(base: &str, sessions: &[String]) -> String` (pure function — takes the session list as input so it is trivially testable)
If `base` is not in `sessions`, return `base` unchanged
Otherwise start `suffix = 2`; while `format!("{base}-{suffix}")` is in `sessions`, `suffix += 1`; return `format!("{base}-{suffix}")`
Unit tests cover the PRD table: [] → 'myapp', ['myapp'] → 'myapp-2', ['myapp','myapp-2'] → 'myapp-3', ['myapp','myapp-2','myapp-5'] → 'myapp-3' (gap-fill)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire main entry point: detect, name, dispatch, exec
userStories.description: As a Zed user, I want `codemux` to detect the multiplexer, compute a session name from CWD, and exec into the multiplexer command.
acceptanceCriteria: src/main.rs reads CWD via `std::env::current_dir()`, computes basename → `sanitize_session_name`
Loads config (US-004), detects multiplexer (US-005)
Resolves auto_attach: env `CODEMUX_AUTO_ATTACH` ('true'|'false') overrides config.auto_attach overrides default `true`
If auto_attach is true and base name already exists in `list_sessions`, use base name; otherwise call `get_unique_session_name` to pick a new name
Builds command string via launcher's `build_command`, then `exec`s it via `$SHELL -l -c <command>` on Unix using `std::os::unix::process::CommandExt::exec` (no lingering codemux parent)
On Windows, spawn via `cmd.exe /C <command>` and propagate the child exit code
Manual smoke test documented in NOTES: running `codemux` in a directory called `test-proj` with tmux installed lands in a tmux session named `test-proj`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Graceful fallback when no multiplexer is installed
userStories.description: As a user without tmux/zellij, I want codemux to still give me a usable terminal with one helpful warning.
acceptanceCriteria: When `detect_multiplexer` returns `None`, print a single-line warning to stderr: `codemux: tmux/zellij not found on PATH — falling back to $SHELL. Install tmux or zellij to enable multiplexer mode.`
Exec the user's default shell: Unix → `$SHELL` env var, fallback `/bin/sh`; Windows → `%COMSPEC%`, fallback `cmd.exe`
Process is replaced via `exec` on Unix; on Windows spawn+wait and propagate exit code
Add a `decide_fallback_shell(env: &HashMap<String,String>) -> String` helper for testability
Unit test verifies the helper returns `$SHELL` when set, `/bin/sh` otherwise (Unix), `%COMSPEC%` when set, `cmd.exe` otherwise (Windows)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add CLI flags: --version, --help, CODEMUX_DEBUG logging
userStories.description: As a user, I want standard CLI ergonomics and an opt-in debug mode for troubleshooting.
acceptanceCriteria: Use `clap` derive API to define a `Cli` struct with `--version` and `--help` (auto-generated)
All other args are forwarded as-is to the spawned shell command (do not error on unknown args)
When env `CODEMUX_DEBUG=1` is set, log the resolved multiplexer, base name, sanitized name, final session name, and full command to stderr — each line prefixed with `[codemux] `
When `CODEMUX_DEBUG` is unset or anything other than `1`, no debug output
Add a `debug_enabled(env: &HashMap<String,String>) -> bool` helper with a unit test
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add Zed integration: .zed/tasks.json + user config docs (fff-gpui pattern)
userStories.description: As a Zed user, I want both a project-local dev task and clear documentation for the two recommended user-level integration patterns, modeled on how fff-gpui ships a `.zed/tasks.json` and documents `tasks.json` + `keymap.json` for users.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `.zed/tasks.json` at the repo root with a 'Build and run codemux' task: command `cargo build --release && ./target/release/codemux`, `hide: on_success`, `save: all` — mirrors fff-gpui's `.zed/tasks.json` convenience pattern
Create `docs/zed-integration.md` documenting the two integration patterns end-to-end:
Option A — `~/.config/zed/settings.json` snippet pointing `terminal.shell.program` at the absolute path of `codemux`

Option B — `~/.config/zed/tasks.json` snippet defining an 'Open codemux terminal' task (use_new_terminal: true, allow_concurrent_runs: false, reveal: always, shell: system) plus a `~/.config/zed/keymap.json` binding (e.g. `cmd-j` → `task::Spawn`) — modeled after fff-gpui's README example
Cross-reference fff-gpui in the docs as the inspiration for the task-based pattern
README at repo root already references both patterns — this story verifies the linked `docs/zed-integration.md` actually exists and matches
Both JSON snippets in the docs are valid JSON (lint with `python3 -m json.tool` or equivalent)
Typecheck passes (cargo check still succeeds; no Rust changes in this story)
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-05-02: Created .zed/tasks.json and docs/zed-integration.md with Option A (settings.json) and Option B (tasks.json + keymap.json) integration patterns, fff-gpui pattern documented, valid JSON verified

userStories.title: Add GitHub Actions CI workflow
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want CI to verify build, lint, format, and tests on macOS, Linux, and Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with a job matrix: `os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]`
Job runs: checkout, setup-rust (stable + clippy + rustfmt), `cargo build --release`, `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`
Cache cargo registry + target dir using `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2`
Workflow runs on push to main and on pull_request
Verify locally: `cargo build --release && cargo test && cargo clippy -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --check` all succeed
Note in README: release-binary workflow is intentionally deferred to v1.1 (see fff-gpui — source build only initially)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Polish README + add 'Differences from vscode-mux' and 'Design philosophy' verification
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a README that walks me through install → configure (both options) → first terminal, and explains why this project is a binary instead of a Zed extension.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify README.md at repo root covers: Why → Design philosophy (binary vs extension, fff-gpui inspiration) → Features → Install (source build) → Zed integration (Option A settings.json + Option B tasks.json+keymap) → Configuration (env + config.toml) → Session naming table → Multiplexer commands table → Project layout → Differences from vscode-mux → Roadmap → Credits (vscode-mux + fff-gpui + Zed) → License
Add `assets/` folder containing a placeholder `demo.gif` (1×1 transparent PNG renamed is OK if no real GIF yet) and reference it from the README's Why section
All internal markdown links (`tasks/prd-zed-mux.md`, `scripts/ralph/prd.json`, `docs/zed-integration.md`, `LICENSE`) resolve to existing files
All external links in README work (vscode-mux, fff-gpui, Zed docs) — eyeball check or `markdown-link-check`
Typecheck passes
